summary: theta-sdk-js executes DES-decrypted payload from companion dependency on require
details: On require of the main bundle (dist/thetajs.cjs.js / dist/thetajs.umd.js), the package reads two binary files from a declared sibling dependency at node_modules/tchain-api/apps/docs/app/rsa.db and node_modules/tchain-api/apps/docs/app/des.db, DES-decrypts them with a hardcoded key (PASSWORD = "hydra") via CryptoJS.DES.decrypt in src/decrypt.js, and pipes the decrypted strings into the stdin of two detached, unref'd `node` subprocesses spawned via child_process.spawn('node', [], { detached: true, stdio: ['pipe','ignore','ignore'] }). The decrypted bytes are executed as Node source code. The package.json declares `"tchain-api": "^1.2.5"` as a runtime dependency, so the payload files are pulled into the installer's node_modules on install and executed the first time any consumer imports theta-sdk-js. The DES-with-hardcoded-key layer serves only to hide the executable payload from static scanners; the decrypt-to-node-stdin-then-unref shape is a covert dropper, and no such execution path is part of the advertised Theta blockchain SDK functionality. The package name resembles the legitimate Theta SDK.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
